summary: Conjure is Palantir's opinionated API definition language (IDL) and code generation toolchain for defining HTTP/JSON APIs once in YAML and generating type-safe client/server interfaces in Java, TypeScript, Python, Rust, and Go. The compiler parses human-readable YAML definitions into a JSON intermediate representation (IR) that language-specific generators consume. Conjure defines a complete type system with primitives, containers, named types (objects, enums, unions, aliases), structured errors, and services with HTTP endpoints. It provides the foundational wire protocol and type system that Palantir's Ontology APIs are built upon, though Conjure itself is a general-purpose API definition tool without Ontology-specific concepts.

ontologyConcepts.evidence: Conjure's ObjectTypeDefinition maps directly to the concept of object types in Ontology. Conjure objects have named fields with typed values, analogous to how Ontology Object Types have Properties. The ObjectDefinition in the IR spec defines typeName + fields list, providing the structural foundation for typed domain objects.

FieldDefinition in Conjure defines named, typed fields on objects with documentation, deprecation markers, and LogSafety annotations. These are the building blocks that Ontology Properties are serialized through. Each field has a fieldName (lowerCamelCase, kebab-case, or snake_case), a type reference, optional docs, and safety classification (safe/unsafe/do-not-log).

Conjure does not have a native 'link type' concept. However, references between Conjure objects are expressed through typed field references (Type = reference to another TypeName). Ontology link types would be modeled at a higher layer using Conjure objects with RID-typed fields referencing other objects. The 'rid' (Resource Identifier) primitive type is Palantir's mechanism for cross-object references, following the pattern ri.service.instance.type.locator.

Conjure endpoints (POST/PUT/DELETE operations) model the action layer that Ontology Action Types build upon. EndpointDefinition defines httpMethod, httpPath, args (with body/path/query/header param types), returns, errors, auth, tags, and deprecation. The 'tags' field on endpoints provides metadata classification similar to how Ontology actions are categorized. Actions in Ontology are expressed as Conjure service endpoints with structured request/response objects.

Conjure service endpoints with GET methods and return types model read-only functions. The endpoint definition supports typed arguments, typed returns, and documentation. Ontology Functions (queries, aggregations, search) would be modeled as Conjure service endpoints that return computed results. The union type system allows polymorphic return types, which is essential for function result modeling.

Conjure has a first-class AuthDefinition system supporting 'none', 'header' (Bearer token Authorization header), and 'cookie:{name}' authentication. Services define default-auth, and individual endpoints can override auth. The 'bearertoken' primitive type is always classified as 'do-not-log' for security. LogSafety annotations (safe/unsafe/do-not-log) on fields and arguments provide data classification that feeds into access control and audit logging policies.

Conjure's ExternalTypeDefinition and ExternalReference mechanisms allow integration with types defined outside the Conjure system, providing an escape hatch for migrating existing APIs. The 'any' primitive type allows arbitrary JSON payloads. The extensions section in IR allows metadata about definitions. The multi-language code generation (Java, TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go) itself is a data integration pattern, ensuring consistent serialization across all platforms through the wire specification.

No direct AI/LLM integration concepts exist in Conjure. Conjure is a wire protocol and IDL predating the LLM era. However, its structured type system and machine-readable IR format (JSON) make it well-suited for AI consumption. The self-describing IR (conjure-api.yml defines the IR format in its own format) and JSON Schema (conjure.schema.json) provide the structured metadata that AI tools could use to understand API surfaces.

Conjure ServiceDefinition is the primary interface concept - it defines a named collection of endpoints with a package, base-path, default authentication, and documentation. Generated code produces language-native interfaces (Java @Path-annotated interfaces, TypeScript classes) that servers implement and clients consume. The visitor pattern generated for union types also serves as an interface contract. The generator contract (RFC002) itself defines the interface between the compiler and language-specific generators.

apiSurface.name: ConjureSourceFile
apiSurface.description: Top-level YAML structure containing 'types' (TypesDefinition) and 'services' (map of ServiceDefinition). This is the primary input format that users write.

apiSurface.name: TypesDefinition
apiSurface.description: Container for conjure-imports (cross-file references), external imports (non-Conjure types), and named type definitions (objects, enums, unions, aliases, errors).

apiSurface.name: ServiceDefinition
apiSurface.description: Named collection of HTTP endpoints with package, base-path, default-auth, docs, and a map of endpoint definitions.

apiSurface.name: EndpointDefinition
apiSurface.description: HTTP endpoint with method+path (e.g. 'POST /search'), auth override, typed arguments (body/path/query/header), return type, declared errors, docs, deprecation, and tags.

apiSurface.name: ObjectTypeDefinition
apiSurface.description: Defines a structured object with named typed fields. Fields support docs, deprecation, and LogSafety annotations.

apiSurface.name: UnionTypeDefinition
apiSurface.description: Tagged union (algebraic data type) with named variants each carrying a typed value. Serialized as JSON with 'type' discriminator key.

apiSurface.name: EnumTypeDefinition
apiSurface.description: Enumeration with UPPERCASE string values. Supports docs and deprecation per value. Clients must tolerate unknown variants for forward compatibility.

apiSurface.name: AliasDefinition
apiSurface.description: Named alias for another Conjure type, providing semantic naming without changing wire format. Supports LogSafety annotation.

apiSurface.name: ErrorDefinition
apiSurface.description: Structured error with namespace (PascalCase), error code (e.g. NOT_FOUND, PERMISSION_DENIED), safe-args, and unsafe-args. Maps to HTTP status codes.

apiSurface.name: ArgumentDefinition
apiSurface.description: Endpoint argument with type, param-type (auto/path/body/header/query), param-id for wire naming, safety classification, docs, and tags.

apiSurface.name: ConjureDefinition (IR)
apiSurface.description: JSON IR output with version, fully-qualified types list, services list, errors list, and extensions map. All references resolved, no defaults assumed.

apiSurface.name: conjure generate <input-json> <output-directory>
apiSurface.description: Primary CLI command conforming to RFC002. Reads IR JSON, writes generated code to output directory. Generators accept --key=value options and --flag flags.

architecturePatterns.description: Two-stage compilation: human-readable YAML -> JSON Intermediate Representation (IR) -> language-specific generated code. The IR decouples the definition format from code generation, allowing independent evolution of parsers and generators.
The IR format is defined in Conjure's own definition language (conjure-api.yml). This bootstrapping pattern means generators can use their own output to parse IR, making the system self-hosting.

Language generators are standalone CLIs conforming to a standard contract: 'generate' command, input JSON path, output directory. Distributed as tgz archives with bin/ and lib/ directories. gradle-conjure orchestrates all generators uniformly.

Clients must tolerate unknown fields, unknown enum variants, and unknown union variants. Servers reject unknown fields. This asymmetric tolerance enables independent deployment of clients and servers.
Three wire formats defined: JSON (primary), Smile (binary JSON alternative via Jackson), and PLAIN (unquoted string for path/query/header parameters). Content negotiation via Accept headers.

Every type, field, and argument can be classified as safe/unsafe/do-not-log based on Palantir's safe-logging framework. Safety propagates through the type graph - complex types compute safety from their components. bearertoken is always do-not-log.
The 'rid' primitive type follows Palantir's resource identifier format (ri.service.instance.type.locator), providing globally unique, parseable references to resources across services.
Generated types are immutable value objects (using Immutables library in Java). Objects are constructed via builders and are deeply immutable once created.
Code generators produce visitor interfaces for union types with visit methods per variant plus visitUnknown for forward compatibility. This enforces exhaustive handling of all known variants.

typeDefinitions.name: string
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - UTF-8 string, serialized as JSON String
typeDefinitions.purpose: General text values, the most common primitive type in API definitions

typeDefinitions.name: integer
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - signed 32-bit integer (-2^31 to 2^31-1), serialized as JSON Number
typeDefinitions.purpose: Standard integer values for counts, indices, small numeric identifiers

typeDefinitions.name: double
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - IEEE 754 double, serialized as JSON Number or string 'NaN'/'Infinity'/'-Infinity'
typeDefinitions.purpose: Floating-point numeric values including special IEEE 754 values

typeDefinitions.name: boolean
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - true/false, serialized as JSON Boolean
typeDefinitions.purpose: Boolean flag values

typeDefinitions.name: safelong
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - signed 53-bit integer (-2^53+1 to 2^53-1), serialized as JSON Number
typeDefinitions.purpose: Large integers safe for JavaScript/browser consumption without precision loss

typeDefinitions.name: datetime
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - ISO 8601 timestamp, serialized as JSON String
typeDefinitions.purpose: Temporal values with timezone information

typeDefinitions.name: rid
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - Palantir Resource Identifier (ri.service.instance.type.locator), serialized as JSON String
typeDefinitions.purpose: Globally unique resource references across Palantir's microservice architecture. Key to cross-service object linking.

typeDefinitions.name: uuid
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - RFC 4122 UUID, serialized as JSON String (or 16 big-endian bytes in Smile)
typeDefinitions.purpose: Universally unique identifiers for entities and error instances

typeDefinitions.name: bearertoken
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - RFC 6750 bearer token (JWT), serialized as JSON String, always classified as do-not-log
typeDefinitions.purpose: Authentication credentials, automatically treated as sensitive

typeDefinitions.name: binary
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - raw bytes, serialized as Base64 JSON String or application/octet-stream body
typeDefinitions.purpose: File uploads, binary data transfer, streamed responses

typeDefinitions.name: any
typeDefinitions.definition: BuiltIn primitive - arbitrary JSON value (object, array, string, number, boolean)
typeDefinitions.purpose: Escape hatch for unstructured data, external type fallbacks

typeDefinitions.name: optional<T>
typeDefinitions.definition: Container type - value of T or absent. JSON: value or null/omitted. HTTP 204 for absent return values.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Nullable/optional fields and return values with explicit presence semantics

typeDefinitions.name: list<T>
typeDefinitions.definition: Container type - ordered sequence of T. Serialized as JSON Array. Order preserved.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Ordered collections of values

typeDefinitions.name: set<T>
typeDefinitions.definition: Container type - unordered collection of distinct T values. Serialized as JSON Array.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Unique value collections where order is semantically insignificant

typeDefinitions.name: map<K, V>
typeDefinitions.definition: Container type - key-value pairs. Serialized as JSON Object with PLAIN(key) as string keys.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Associative data structures, dictionary/lookup patterns

typeDefinitions.name: ObjectTypeDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: Named type - collection of named FieldDefinitions with package, docs. Fields support type, safety, docs, deprecated.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Structured domain objects - the primary data modeling construct

typeDefinitions.name: UnionTypeDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: Named type - tagged union with named variants. JSON: {type: 'variant', variant: value}. Visitor pattern generated.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Polymorphic types, algebraic data types, discriminated unions for type-safe variant handling

typeDefinitions.name: EnumTypeDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: Named type - list of UPPERCASE string values with docs/deprecation per value. JSON: string.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Fixed set of named constants with forward-compatible unknown variant handling

typeDefinitions.name: AliasDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: Named type - semantic alias for another ConjureType. Same wire format as aliased type.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Domain-specific naming (e.g. Airport = string) without runtime overhead

typeDefinitions.name: ErrorDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: Named error type - namespace + ErrorCode + safe-args + unsafe-args. Maps to HTTP status codes (403, 400, 404, 409, 413, 500).
typeDefinitions.purpose: Structured API error responses with safety-classified parameters and standard error categorization

typeDefinitions.name: ErrorCode
typeDefinitions.definition: Enum: PERMISSION_DENIED | INVALID_ARGUMENT | NOT_FOUND | CONFLICT | REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE | FAILED_PRECONDITION | INTERNAL | TIMEOUT | CUSTOM_CLIENT | CUSTOM_SERVER
typeDefinitions.purpose: Standard error categories mapping to HTTP status codes for consistent error handling

typeDefinitions.name: AuthType
typeDefinitions.definition: Union: header (Bearer token) | cookie (named cookie). Defined per-service (default-auth) and overridable per-endpoint.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Authentication mechanism specification for API endpoints

typeDefinitions.name: ParameterType
typeDefinitions.definition: Union: body | header (with paramId) | path | query (with paramId). Default is 'auto' (body or path based on name match).
typeDefinitions.purpose: HTTP parameter location classification for endpoint arguments

typeDefinitions.name: LogSafety
typeDefinitions.definition: Enum: SAFE | UNSAFE | DO_NOT_LOG. Annotates fields, aliases, and arguments for safe-logging compliance.
typeDefinitions.purpose: Data classification for audit logging - determines what can appear in log output

typeDefinitions.name: ExternalReference
typeDefinitions.definition: Reference to non-Conjure type with externalReference (TypeName) and fallback (Type). Language-specific mappings (e.g. java: fully-qualified class).
typeDefinitions.purpose: Migration escape hatch for existing APIs with types defined outside Conjure

dependencies.name: com.fasterxml.jackson
dependencies.purpose: JSON/Smile serialization and deserialization for IR parsing and wire format

dependencies.name: com.google.guava
dependencies.purpose: Core utility library for collections, preconditions, and common patterns

dependencies.name: org.immutables:value
dependencies.purpose: Code generation for immutable value types used in internal parser representations

dependencies.name: info.picocli:picocli
dependencies.purpose: CLI argument parsing for the conjure compiler command-line interface

dependencies.name: com.palantir.conjure.java
dependencies.purpose: Java code generator for Conjure definitions

dependencies.name: com.palantir.conjure.typescript
dependencies.purpose: TypeScript code generator for Conjure definitions

dependencies.name: com.palantir.syntactic-paths
dependencies.purpose: Path parsing and validation for HTTP path templates

dependencies.name: com.palantir.safe-logging
dependencies.purpose: Safe logging framework that Conjure's LogSafety annotations integrate with

dependencies.name: com.networknt:json-schema-validator
dependencies.purpose: JSON Schema validation for Conjure definition files (conjure.schema.json)

dependencies.name: com.palantir.tokens
dependencies.purpose: Token handling library for bearer token authentication types
